title: Stranger Things Season 5 — Everything We Know About the Final Season
excerpt: The Duffer Brothers are ending Stranger Things with Season 5 — and everything we know so far suggests it's going to be the biggest, darkest chapter yet.
author: StarScoop Daily Staff
metaDescription: Stranger Things Season 5 is the final chapter — release date, cast, plot details, and everything we know about Netflix's biggest finale in 2026.
content:
The Final Season Is Almost Here

After nearly a decade, Stranger Things is coming to an end. Season 5 is the confirmed final chapter of one of Netflix's biggest shows ever — and the Duffer Brothers have promised it will be worth the wait.

The show first premiered in 2016 and turned Hawkins, Indiana into the most famous fictional small town in pop culture. Now, with 42 episodes across four seasons, the story of Eleven, the Upside Down, and the gang is heading toward its conclusion.

Whether you've been watching from the beginning or you're just catching up, here's everything confirmed about Season 5 so far.

What Happened at the End of Season 4?

Season 4 ended with Hawkins literally cracking open. Vecna (Henry Creel / One / 001) triggered four massive gates across the town, causing widespread destruction. Max fell into a coma after Vecna nearly killed her, and Eleven was only able to briefly bring her back.

The Upside Down started bleeding into the real world in a terrifying way — ash falling like snow, vines creeping through the streets. The gang is scattered, battered, and finally back together — but Hawkins is in ruins.

Season 5 picks up directly where Season 4 left off. No time jump this time.

Cast — Who's Coming Back?

The core cast is all returning for the final season. Millie Bobby Brown (Eleven), David Harbour (Hopper), Winona Ryder (Joyce), Finn Wolfhard (Mike), Gaten Matarazzo (Dustin), Caleb McLaughlin (Lucas), Noah Schnapp (Will), and Sadie Sink (Max) are all confirmed.

The Duffer Brothers have confirmed some new faces will appear — though they've kept details tight. Given the scale of the finale, expect surprise returns and major character moments for everyone.

Plot — What Will Season 5 Be About?

The Duffer Brothers have compared Season 5's tone to a war movie. After seasons of running and surviving, the gang is finally going on the offensive against Vecna and the Upside Down.

Key questions Season 5 needs to answer: Will Max wake up from her coma? What is Will's connection to Vecna now? Will Eleven lose her powers again? And most importantly — can Hawkins actually be saved?

The brothers have also confirmed that the ending has been planned since Season 1. Every clue, every detail — it all leads to this.

Release Date and Where to Watch

Stranger Things Season 5 is exclusively on Netflix. Netflix has not yet confirmed the exact premiere date, but production wrapped in 2025 and a 2026 release is expected.

Seasons 1–4 are all available on Netflix right now if you need to catch up before the finale drops. With 42 episodes already in the bank, it's the perfect binge before the final chapter.

Why This Ending Matters

Stranger Things didn't just become a hit show — it defined an era of Netflix and brought '80s nostalgia back to mainstream culture. The finale has to deliver for millions of fans who've grown up alongside these characters.

The Duffer Brothers have said they cried writing the finale. That's either a very good sign or a very bad sign for your favourite characters — possibly both.
